essence: Provides immediate lifesaving care in the critical minutes before ambulance crews arrive.

identity.title: Community First Responder (Volunteer Emergency Responder)
identity.summary: Attends local 999 medical emergencies, delivers basic life support, and stabilises patients until paramedics arrive.

salary.notes: Typically a volunteer role; some organisations offer stipends or paid responder roles in advanced schemes.

qualifications: First responder or basic life support training
Knowledge of CPR, AED use, and trauma basics
Ability to remain calm and reassuring under pressure

lifestyle.description: Community first responders attend local medical emergencies such as cardiac arrests, breathing difficulties, and trauma incidents. They provide immediate care, reassure patients, and support paramedics on arrival. The role suits individuals who want to contribute directly to public safety, often alongside another career.

habits: Responds quickly and safely to callouts
